notes:
dữ = fierce, aggressive, mean. It describes someone or something that feels scary, strict, or intense.

It’s an adjective, used after the noun: animals (chó dữ), people (người dữ), facial expressions or tone (mặt dữ, giọng dữ). Stronger form: hung dữ (very violent). Opposite: hiền (gentle, kind).

In the South, dữ is also used at the end of a sentence to mean “very” or “so” (strong feeling).

Structure (Southern): Adj/Verb + dữ! (đẹp dữ!, đông dữ!)
notes_md:
**dữ** = fierce, aggressive, mean. It describes someone or something that feels scary, strict, or intense.

It’s an **adjective**, used after the noun: animals (chó dữ), people (người dữ), facial expressions or tone (mặt dữ, giọng dữ). Stronger form: **hung dữ** (very violent). Opposite: **hiền** (gentle, kind).

In the South, **dữ** is also used at the end of a sentence to mean “very” or “so” (strong feeling).

Structure (Southern): Adj/Verb + dữ! (đẹp dữ!, đông dữ!)
sentenceBreakdowns: Con (classifier for animals) + chó (dog) + dữ (fierce) + cắn (bites) + anh ấy (him)
Cô giáo (teacher) + dữ (fierce) + phạt (punishes) + em ấy (him)
exampleSentences: Con chó dữ cắn anh ấy.
Cô giáo dữ phạt em ấy.
